# Kineticist Class Feats — Levels 1–2 (PF2e) — Batch 2

Complete reference of Kineticist class feats from levels 1–2 (second batch: Metal, Water, Wood element impulses + general class feats).

Sources: [Archives of Nethys](https://2e.aonprd.com/) — Rage of Elements pg. 21, 24, 26, 28, 30, 32, 34

---

## Level 1 (14 feats)

### Hardwood Armor
- **ID:** 4283 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Impulse, Kineticist, Primal, Wood
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 34
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Wood and bark grow over your body like armor. This is medium armor, and you can use your highest armor proficiency while wearing it.
  - **Armor Stats:** AC Bonus +3, Dex Cap +2, Check Penalty –2, Speed Penalty –5 ft, Strength +2, Bulk 1, Group wood.
  - This armor suppresses the effects of any actual armor you wear, but any runes on that armor that could apply to the wood armor are replicated on it.
  - You can also create a wooden shield in a free hand, which uses the statistics of a regular wooden shield. You can Shield Block with this shield even if you don't have that feat. The hand holding the shield counts as a free hand for using impulses. If the shield is destroyed (broken), or you aren't holding it at the start of your turn, it decays to nothing.
  - The impulse lasts for 10 minutes, and you can Dismiss it. If you use this impulse again, any previous use ends.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+3):** The shield's Hardness increases by 1, its HP by 4, and its BT by 2.
- **Special:** —

### Magnetic Pinions
- **ID:** 4252 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Attack, Impulse, Kineticist, Metal, Overflow, Primal
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 30
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Small pieces of metal fly from you, propelled with magnetism. Make ranged impulse attack rolls against up to 3 creatures within 60 feet. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll against a target wearing metal armor or a target made of metal. All three attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. Each attack that hits deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage and 1d4 piercing damage. On a critical hit, the damage is doubled.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** Each type of damage increases by 1d4.
- **Special:** —

### Metal Carapace
- **ID:** 4253 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Impulse, Kineticist, Metal, Primal
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 30
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Sheets of bent and rusted metal cover you like armor. This is medium armor, and you can use your highest armor proficiency while wearing it.
  - **Armor Stats:** AC Bonus +3, Dex Cap +2, Check Penalty –2, Speed Penalty –5 ft, Strength +2, Bulk 2, Group plate, Traits noisy.
  - This armor suppresses the effects of any actual armor you wear, but any runes on that armor that could apply to the metal armor are replicated on it.
  - You can also create a rusty steel shield in a free hand, which uses the statistics of a regular steel shield. You can Shield Block with this shield even if you don't have that feat. The hand holding the shield counts as a free hand for using impulses. If the shield is destroyed (broken), or you aren't holding it at the start of your turn, it crumbles to nothing.
  - The impulse lasts for 10 minutes, and you can Dismiss it. If you use this impulse again, any previous use ends.
  - The metal is rusted and fragile. If you take damage from a critical hit, the metal shatters and the impulse ends.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+3):** The shield's Hardness increases by 1, its HP by 4, and its BT by 2.
- **Special:** —

### Ocean's Balm
- **ID:** 4267 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Healing, Impulse, Kineticist, Manipulate, Primal, Vitality, Water
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 32
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** A blessing of the living sea restores a creature. Touch a willing living creature. It regains 1d8 Hit Points and gains resistance 2 to fire for 1 minute. If the creature has persistent fire damage, it can attempt a flat check to remove it with the especially appropriate help modifier. After a creature is healed by Ocean's Balm, it is temporarily immune to healing from Ocean's Balm for 10 minutes.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** The healing increases by 1d8, and the resistance increases by 1.
- **Special:** —

### Scorching Column
- **ID:** 4239 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Fire, Impulse, Kineticist, Overflow, Primal
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 28
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** A vertical column of extreme heat rises from the ground. The column is a cylinder 10 feet in diameter and 30 feet high, and the bottom of the cylinder must be within 60 feet. Each creature in the area takes 1d6 fire damage with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. The flame remains; all the squares in the area become hazardous terrain until the end of your next turn. You can Sustain the impulse up to 1 minute. A creature takes 1 fire damage each time it moves into one of these squares.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+3):** The initial damage increases by 1d6, and the hazardous terrain damage increases by 2.
- **Special:** —

### Shard Strike
- **ID:** 4254 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Impulse, Kineticist, Metal, Primal
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 30
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Jagged metal shards form and lash out. Choose one of the following options. Each creature in the area attempts a basic Reflex save against your class DC.
  - **Shards:** 1d6 slashing damage in a 15-foot cone. On a critical failure, a creature also takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage.
  - **Spines:** 1d6 piercing damage in a 30-foot line. On a critical failure, a creature is also clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** The damage increases by 1d6.
- **Special:** —

### Stepping Stones
- **ID:** 4223 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Earth, Impulse, Kineticist, Manipulate, Primal
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 26
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Disks of rock fly to designated points to create a new path. Set them horizontally across a horizontal surface or liquid, creating a path up to 20 feet long (4 squares), or anchor them into a vertical surface, creating a set of steps up to 10 feet high. All squares must be contiguous and within 60 feet. A creature moving across the stepping stones can ignore any difficult terrain or hazardous terrain created by the ground beneath them. The stones last until the end of your next turn, and you can Sustain the impulse.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** Increase the path length by 10 feet or the step height by 5 feet, and increase the maximum distance by 10 feet.
- **Special:** —

### Tidal Hands
- **ID:** 4268 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Impulse, Kineticist, Overflow, Primal, Water
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 32
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** You create waves rushing out in the shape of hands. You create either two 15-foot cones that don't overlap or one 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area takes 1d8 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. On a critical failure, a creature is also pushed 5 feet.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** The damage increases by 1d8.
- **Special:** —

### Timber Sentinel
- **ID:** 4284 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Impulse, Kineticist, Plant, Primal, Wood
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 34
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** A slim, symmetrical tree travels from the Plane of Wood to stand guard. You conjure a tree in an unoccupied square within 30 feet. The tree acts as a protector tree spell of a rank equal to half your level rounded up. If you use this impulse again, any previous one ends, and an ordinary (non-magical) tree remains.
- **Level Entries:**
  - Scales via the protector tree spell rank (half your level, rounded up).
- **Special:** —

### Tremor
- **ID:** 4224 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Earth, Impulse, Kineticist, Overflow, Primal
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 26
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** You create a small, localized tremor in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet. Each creature in the area takes 1d8 bludgeoning damage with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC. On a critical failure, a creature is also knocked prone. The earth and stone in the area become difficult terrain until the start of your next turn.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** The damage increases by 1d8.
- **Special:** —

### Versatile Blasts
- **ID:** 4185 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Kineticist
- **Action:** — (passive)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 21
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Accessing a wider realm of elemental manifestations, you can make blasts with rare forms of your elements. Add the following damage types to those you can choose for Elemental Blasts of that element:
  - **Air:** cold
  - **Earth:** poison
  - **Fire:** cold
  - **Metal:** electricity
  - **Water:** acid
  - **Wood:** poison
- **Level Entries:** —
- **Special:** —

### Weapon Infusion
- **ID:** 4186 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Infusion, Kineticist
- **Action:** ⭓ (free action)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 21
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** With a signature flair, you shape blasts of elemental power into the form of a weapon, such as a sword made of whirling wind or a bow that shoots flames. If your next action is an Elemental Blast, choose a weapon shape for it to take. You can choose to change the blast's damage type to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing—whichever suits the weapon shape—and you can choose other alterations depending on whether you make a melee or ranged blast.
  - **Melee:** Add one of the following traits of your choice: agile, backswing, forceful, reach, sweep.
  - **Ranged:** Choose one of three options: range increment 100 feet and the volley 30 feet trait; range increment 50 feet and the propulsive trait; or range increment 20 feet and the deadly d8 trait.
- **Level Entries:** —
- **Special:** —

### Whisper on the Wind
- **ID:** 4209 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Air, Auditory, Illusion, Impulse, Kineticist, Linguistic, Primal
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 24
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** You speak in a whisper, a soft wind carrying your words far away. This has the effect of the message spell with a range of 500 feet, and it can target only a creature surrounded by air. If you start your message with the target's name, you and the target don't need to be able to see each other, nor do you need line of effect.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (4th):** The range is 1 mile.
  - **Level (14th):** The range is planetary plus the Plane of Air.
- **Special:** —

### Winter's Clutch
- **ID:** 4269 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Cold, Impulse, Kineticist, Primal, Water
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 32
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Gleaming flakes of chilling snow fall in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet. Each creature in the area takes 2d4 cold damage with a basic Reflex save against your class DC. The ground in the area is covered in a snow drift, which is difficult terrain. Each square of the drift lasts until it melts, either naturally or until fire damage is dealt in that square.
- **Level Entries:**
  - **Level (+2):** The damage increases by 1d4.
- **Special:** —

---

## Level 2 (2 feats)

### Kinetic Activation
- **ID:** 4187 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Kineticist
- **Action:** — (passive)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 21
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** Rather than incantations and gestures, you can use your kinetic gate to directly unlock the potential of elementally empowered magic items. You can Activate magic items that require you to be able to Cast a Spell, provided you activate them to Cast a Spell with the same trait as one of your kinetic elements; for example, if you can channel fire, you could Activate a scroll of fireball. For any effects of these items that use a spell attack roll or spell DC, you can substitute your impulse attack roll or class DC.
  - You can also prepare a staff that has at least one spell with an appropriate trait, using half your level rounded up to determine the number of charges you add. This doesn't allow you to cast spells without the trait of one of your kinetic elements, and you don't get the extra benefits prepared and spontaneous spellcasters do.
- **Level Entries:** —
- **Special:** —

### Voice of the Elements
- **ID:** 4188 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Kineticist
- **Action:** — (passive)
- **Source:** Rage of Elements pg. 21 (also Draconic Codex pg. 216)
- **Prerequisites:** —
- **Requirements:** —
- **Frequency:** —
- **Description:** You can speak with the secret tones of elements you channel, finding words in the crackle of flame, the grinding of stone. Your kinetic aura grants you these three benefits while active:
  - You can speak all the languages of your kinetic elements (Sussuran for air, Petran for earth, Pyric for fire, Talican for metal, Thalassic for water, Muan for wood).
  - You can communicate with mindless elementals on a basic level if they have a trait that matches one of your kinetic elements or are made of one of those elements. This allows you to use Diplomacy to Make an Impression on them and to make very simple Requests.
  - You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Charisma-based skill checks you attempt against elementals of one of your kinetic elements.
- **Level Entries:** —
- **Special:** —
